body.title: Register the coding domain's full Ollama ladder: 2 Hex-local + 2 Ollama-Cloud flagships
body.description:
**Problem:** the coding domain's difficulty ladder is incomplete. Today: code=qwen2.5-coder:14b(Hex,$0)+devstral-small-2:24b(Hex/cloud,$0); design=EMPTY (no coding-domain model at all). qwen3-coder:30b and deepseek-r1:32b are already pulled on Hex (this session) but not registered; the Ollama Cloud subscription flagships (qwen3-coder:480b-cloud, deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud -- too large for Hex's 32GB) are not registered at all. Corrected design (D-coding-domain-hex-cloud-ladder-2026-07-01): the coding ladder is Qwen(code)->DeepSeek(design), Hex-local first, Ollama-Cloud subscription only for the flagships Hex cannot hold. No OR/Anthropic/Google belongs on this domain's ladder.

**CRITICAL WIRING GAP FOUND (must be in this ticket, not a follow-up):** route()'s candidate pool is filtered FIRST by `rule.task_class == task_class` (rules_engine.py ~L222), BEFORE required_difficulty is ever considered. DS ALWAYS calls dispatch with task_class='worker' (tier-not-model contract, T-router-failure-bump-escalation) -- it never sends task_class='designer' or 'analyst'. So a new RoutingRule filed under task_class='designer' or 'analyst' would be INVISIBLE to DS's escalation walk no matter what required_difficulty says -- the walk would bump to 'design' difficulty and find zero candidates, halting on a false capability-ceiling even though a perfectly good local architect exists. ALL FOUR new rules must be filed under task_class='worker' (matching what DS actually sends); the model's difficulty_capable field (not the rule's task_class) is what makes the escalation walk find them at the design rung. This matches the existing pattern (Hex code-tier rules are already task_class='worker').

**Shape:** models_registry.py -- add 4 ModelSpecs, all domains=['coding'], dollars=0:
  1. qwen3-coder:30b, source='ollama' (Hex), tier='worker', difficulty_capable='code', features=['tools']
  2. deepseek-r1:32b, source='ollama' (Hex), tier='analyst', difficulty_capable='design' -- the local architect, fills the empty design slot
  3. qwen3-coder:480b-cloud, source='ollama_cloud', tier='worker', difficulty_capable='code' -- cloud escalation target, too large for Hex
  4. deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud, source='ollama_cloud', tier='analyst', difficulty_capable='design' -- cloud architect, too large for Hex
rules_engine.py -- add 4 RoutingRules, ALL task_class='worker' (per the wiring gap above): 2 for source='ollama' (Hex, cost_class=owned_local, cheapest -- wins over cloud when Hex is up), 2 for source='ollama_cloud' (cost_class=subscription, wins only when Hex is down/insufficient).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/models_registry.py, unseen_university/devices/inference/rules_engine.py, tests/inference/test_hex_registration.py (extend) or a new test_coding_domain_ladder.py.

**Design rules:** route by {domain, task_class, required_difficulty}; cost-ordered selector (owned_local Hex beats subscription cloud); NO SQLITE; domain routing keeps generalist providers (Claude/Gemini/OR) out of this domain entirely.

**Scope boundary:** IN -- the 4 ModelSpecs + 4 RoutingRules (all task_class='worker') + tests proving the design-difficulty rung is now reachable by DS's actual call shape. OUT -- removing the existing OR-tagged coding entries (qwen/qwen3-coder-30b-a3b-instruct, qwen/qwen3-coder -- inert since OR is disabled, cleanup is a separate lower-priority ticket); the escalation-ladder 'exhaust family before advancing' refinement (T-inference-escalation-ladder-family-then-architect, separate); fixing the same task_class-visibility gap for the OTHER pre-existing analyst/designer-tier rules that DS also can't reach (pre-existing, out of scope, note only).

**Test plan (stub-first, model-agnostic where possible):** (1) registry has qwen3-coder:30b and qwen3-coder:480b-cloud at difficulty_capable=code, domains=coding; (2) registry has deepseek-r1:32b and deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud at difficulty_capable=design, domains=coding; (3) route(task_class='worker', domain='coding', required_difficulty='design') with Hex available returns source='ollama' model='deepseek-r1:32b' (NOT the cloud one -- cost_class ordering); (4) same call with 'ollama' unavailable returns source='ollama_cloud' model='deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud'; (5) route(task_class='worker', domain='coding', required_difficulty='code') still prefers the cheapest Hex coder over the 480b cloud one when Hex is up; (6) neither new design-difficulty model is reachable via task_class='designer' or 'analyst' surprises -- confirms the fix targets DS's actual call shape.

**Completion criteria:** all 4 tests green; a live Hex dispatch at required_difficulty='design' (task_class=worker) routes to deepseek-r1:32b/ollama, verified against the real box.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Registered the coding domain's Ollama ladder: qwen3-coder:30b (Hex/code) + deepseek-r1:32b (Hex/DESIGN — fills the empty design rung) + qwen3-coder:480b-cloud + deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud (Ollama Cloud flagships too large for Hex). All 4 RoutingRules under task_class='worker' so DS's escalation walk (which always sends task_class='worker' + required_difficulty) can actually reach the design rung — fixed the wiring gap where a designer/analyst rule would be invisible to DS. Intention reworded to lowest-cost-with-required-capabilities (a cheaper capable generalist still legitimately wins; nothing excluded). 6 new tests green; full inference suite only the 3 pre-existing mini-rack fails; live-confirmed Hex serving all 6 models.
body.unproven_reason: The routing behaviors (local-first, cheapest-capable-when-Hex-down, cloud-flagship-when-cheapest-left, worker-task_class-visibility) are pinned by 6 additive tests the proof_emitter can't diff-invert (T-proof-emitter-additive-surface-mode). Live behavioral proof — a stuck coding task actually bumping code→design onto deepseek-r1:32b — is rung 3, still to run.
